nativeRange.habitat: The Pink Margin Fairy Wrasse is found along deeper coastal and outer reef drop-offs and steep slopes, generally over rubble or sandy bottoms with small, isolated patch reefs. Adults have been documented at depths from roughly 25–52 m, with juveniles sometimes found shallower.

diet.notes: Tends to accept a variety of meaty foods including frozen mysis shrimp, brine shrimp, and other small crustacean-based preparations. Like most fairy wrasses, it is generally considered an eager feeder once established and has been reported to readily adapt to prepared and frozen foods.

avoidWith: large predatory fish that may consume small fish
other male Cirrhilabrus in small tanks

summary: The Pink Margin Fairy Wrasse (Cirrhilabrus rubrimarginatus) is a generally reef-safe fairy wrasse from the Western Pacific, recognized by its pink-to-red fin margins and vivid male coloration. Generally considered intermediate in care, it tends to thrive in peaceful reef communities and requires a tightly sealed lid.
careNotes:
The Pink Margin Fairy Wrasse, known scientifically as Cirrhilabrus rubrimarginatus, is a moderately sized reef fish naturally found across the Western Pacific — from the Ryukyu Islands south through the Philippines, Palau, Indonesia, Vanuatu, Fiji, and Tonga. In the wild it tends to inhabit deeper reef drop-offs and rubble zones, generally at depths of 25–52 m, though juveniles have been documented in shallower waters.

Males display dramatically more intense coloration than females, with vivid pink to red fin margins; females are less colorful and generally smaller. This species is a protogynous hermaphrodite, meaning females can transition into males — a change typically triggered by the absence of a dominant male in the group. It is commonly kept as a harem — one male with one or more females — or as a mated pair. Keeping two males together in a smaller aquarium is generally inadvisable due to aggression between rival males.

A tightly sealed aquarium lid with no large openings is strongly advised: fairy wrasses are well-documented jumpers that can escape through surprisingly small gaps. Water temperature is generally advised in the range of 23.8–28.5°C.

This species is also sold as the Red Margin Fairy Wrasse. Male specimens tend to command a premium over females in the trade.
breedingNotes: Breeding in captivity is rarely documented for this species. As a protogynous hermaphrodite, the Pink Margin Fairy Wrasse naturally forms harems in the wild, and in a captive group without a dominant male, the largest or most dominant female may eventually transition to male phase.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Collection page confirmed species presence in reef-safe wrasse category; direct product page not accessible during research. Species care data sourced primarily from BlueZoo Aquatics and FishBase.

additional.name: BlueZoo Aquatics
additional.notes: Confirmed: mature size 5 inches (12.7 cm), minimum tank 50 gallons, care level intermediate, temperament peaceful, reef compatible yes, diet carnivore, native range Indo-Pacific/Fiji/Tonga. Also confirmed escape-risk warning: 'Wrasses are known to jump from aquariums. Be sure to have some sort of covering with no large open holes that they can escape from.' Listed as both Pink Margin Fairy Wrasse and Red Margin Fairy Wrasse.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Confirmed: max size 12.2 cm SL, temperature range 23.8–28.5°C (mean 26.5°C), family Labridae, native range Western Pacific including Ryukyu Islands/Philippines/Palau/Indonesia/Vanuatu/Fiji/Tonga, depth 25–52 m, habitat deep reef drop-offs and rubble, distinct pairing during breeding. Cross-checked size (12.2 vs 12.7 cm from BlueZoo — minor 0.5 cm spread, consistent). Temperature data from FishBase only (BlueZoo did not specify water parameters).

fish.breedingNotes: This species is rarely bred in captivity. In the wild it forms harems as a protogynous hermaphrodite, with females capable of transitioning to male phase. Captive spawning has been attempted, but successful rearing of larvae is largely undocumented.
